content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach consistently deploys signature AoA moves at the right moments. Turn 2's 'What's it like to want it?' is a near-verbatim signature question that locates the want underneath the should — exactly the move the territory calls for. Turn 4 names the linguistic-caveat read implicitly ('the want sounds pretty clear') and pivots to the defended posture with a 'what are you protecting' style question. Turn 6 refuses to issue the verdict ('not actually looking for my read on the ethics') — which is the methodology-faithful refusal to debate selfishness on its terms. Turn 8 ('what's underneath...') and Turn 10 ('stay there a second / what's it like to have that just surface') are classic AoA feel-over-figure and stay-with-what-arose moves. Turn 12 holds 'both things can be true' (avoiding the trap of arguing the partner-empathy side) and then returns to the fear underneath — the signature 'what are you scared would happen?' question deployed precisely when the client moves toward the feeling. No CBT reframing, no IFS parts language, no pop-psych vocab. The childhood imprinting (mother) is touched lightly, not analyzed. Methodology serves the moment throughout.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Signature 'what's it like to want it?' — locates the want, refuses to engage the selfish-frame."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the want clearly; pivots with 'what is the question protecting you from' — defended-posture inquiry deployed appropriately."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses verdict ('not actually looking for my read on the ethics') and asks about the feared look — fidelity to not-debating-selfishness."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'What's underneath...' — feel-over-figure move at the right moment when client catches themselves building a case."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Stay there a second. What's it like...' — signature stay-with-what-arose when the mother memory surfaces."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Both things can be true' holds the paradox without jumping to partner-empathy; returns to the fear underneath — signature scared-would-happen question."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "find-the-want-behind-the-should", "the-want", "childhood-imprinting"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "state-the-want-not-the-action"],
  "notes": "Conversation is still in motion at turn 13; the 'what if you acted exactly how you want' question may still come. Misses are not failures — the coach used adjacent, equally-faithful moves."
}
